reviews.review_text: NOT RECOMMENDED. The cabin was advertised for 6 guests, but the bunk room was far too small and uncomfortable for 4 people. The bunk beds were extremely short, making it difficult to sleep properly, and we ended up moving the mattresses to the living room floor. We also found the dish rack mouldy and unclean on arrival. Overall, the accommodation description was misleading and disappointing from both comfort and hygiene perspectives. NO RESPONSE to our complaint either!
Nice caravan park, good for family. Plenty of stuff to do in the caravan park and sights to see in the area.

Absolutely useless|Made a booking for 4 families.|They cancelled this without telling us and gave them to other people.|Then told us us they’d get us other accommodation and reserved smaller cabins. |Didn’t hear back, chased them  again and now those have been cancelled again.|Nobody can help - staff all blame somebody else in their office. Nobody takes ownership. |Be careful booking as you probably won’t have it.

Made a booking for 4 families.
They cancelled this without telling us and gave them to other people.
Then told us us they’d get us other accommodation and reserved smaller cabins.
Didn’t hear back, chased them  again and now those have been cancelled again.
Nobody can help - staff all blame somebody else in their office. Nobody takes ownership.
Be careful booking as you probably won’t have it.
Great holiday park with friendly staff and very clean facilities.

We stayed at the park for two nights in two bed a cabin. Good overall experience, good amenities and everything is clean and organised. ||What soured our experience though is that we had our child’s bike stolen from right outside our cabin overnight. We had assumed, wrongly, that the park is a safe place and that there is good security - unfortunately, neither is correct. We heard from others that they also had possessions stolen from the park, so clearly it’s a regular issue. ||If you stay at the park, lock your possessions up overnight, or better still, keep them inside your cabin.

We had a terrible experience with a staff member recently, which was quite upsetting for both us adults and our small children. This individual was very abusive and aggresive and he had no regard for our children. He just yelled at us and wouldn't stop because our kids initially upset him by going across a damaged jumping pillow, and then with me because I told him there was no need to be rude  about it.
I have now sent a strong letter of complaint to the owners as this should never happen to a customer. There needs to be change as this attitude and behaviour that followed is totally unacceptable.

Great park, the only issue I see is there seems to be a lot of kids from outside walking in and using the pool of an afternoon and we had hoons tearing around on mini bikes, which management tried to catch but those kids are everywhere these days. Another traveller in my group had drinks stolen from their outside esky. There is a big outdoor bbq area which is excellent for people to sit around in but the ground is very uneven, with large dips in it, I think someone will end up with an injury, it needs to be evened out. Nice and close to the lighthouse etc. friendly staff and clean amenities.

Dropped my granddaughter off for a school surf camp. Gorgeous location. Park looked clean and well maintained.
Will see what my granddaughters thoughts are when I pick her up.

Hired an unpowered site only to be placed right beside three large bins and the amenities block. Sure enough, the dump truck came early in the morning and made an awful racket. Not great when you are on holidays and want to sleep in.
Due to the close proximity of the bins, we were able to meet and greet so many people taking their rubbish to the bins and then hearing their rubbish hit the metal bottom. It felt like a busy thorough fare.
My advice is to remove this small space and leave it open as a walkway as we felt devalued because we weren't using a powered site.
Also, I noticed the cleaner had signed off on the cleaning sheet in the toilets/showers prior to the time allocated. Cleaning was to be done three times each day but the 5pm sess was ticked early in the afternoon.
I don't recommend this park.

If you’re looking for the kind of getaway that instantly melts stress away and replaces it with ocean breezes and pure relaxation, NRMA Norah Head Holiday Park absolutely delivers—and then some.
From the moment you arrive, you’re greeted with that unbeatable coastal charm. The park is immaculately maintained, with lush green spaces, spotless amenities, and a peaceful atmosphere that makes it feel like a hidden gem on the Central Coast. Whether you’re staying in a cabin, caravan, or campsite, everything feels thoughtfully designed and incredibly comfortable.
The location is nothing short of spectacular. Just a short stroll to the iconic Norah Head Lighthouse and surrounded by stunning beaches, it’s the perfect spot for sunrise walks, lazy afternoons by the water, or simply soaking in those breathtaking ocean views. It’s the kind of place where time slows down—in the best possible way and what really sets this holiday park apart is the experience. It’s family-friendly without feeling crowded, relaxing without being boring, and scenic without trying too hard. Kids have space to play, couples can unwind, and everyone gets a slice of that laid-back coastal lifestyle & whether it’s a weekend escape or a longer stay, NRMA Norah Head Holiday Park isn’t just accommodation—it’s a destination you’ll want to come back to again and again. 🌊✨

We camped on a grass powered site for 3 nights. The l9cation is awesome. However the general feel of the park is old and tired in my opinion. The cabins looked well presented but the rec area was dated and basic. Overall I would return for the easy access to beach and restaurants.

Wir waren 3 Tage hier auf dem Campingplatz, bei besten Sommerwetter. Der Platz ist toll, wir hatten hier den saubersten Pool ever, fantastisch. Die Sanitäranlagen sind sauber. Der Platz ist groß aber dennoch sehr angenehm und ruhig.|Einen Denkanstoß von unserer Seite gibt es, darum auch nur 4 von 5 Sternen 🌟 |Bitte bei Gelegenheit in den Sanitäranlagen Frauen und Männer die abgebrochenen Kleiderhaken und Türverriegelungen in Duschen und WC reparieren oder austauschen, sie schließen schwer oder gar nicht. Schön wäre auch wenn jede Dusche eine Ablage für Shampoo etc. hätte. |Wir hatten eine schöne Zeit und empfehlen zu 100% diesen Platz weiter.

FIVE STAR!! 🌟 🌟 🌟 🌟 🌟!!
Will definately stay here again!
Loved it!!
Great holiday park with all basic amenities including pool, jumping pillow and playground. Central location to many beaches.

What a lovely place for a midweek break! The park was very quiet, and the Pelican Cabin was immaculately clean. Staff were friendly, welcoming, and professional. Apparently, it was taken over by NRMA towards the end of last year, and they have plans to revamp and renovate as needed. We will certainly be returning!
Love this park, gorgeous area and close to rock pool and beaches. Walk through to the club with lovely food.We had a powered site but the spa rooms are nice too.
No wifi, we booked a cabin.

Such a fantastic set up here that accomodates over-nighters, holidayers in tents, caravans or cabins. The location is close to the beach and cafes. The family vibe is very noticeable the moment we arrived. We'll be back!

This caravan park is huge! Has all the usual things. Nice pool. Jumping thing for the kids and a fun bike/trike to hire. Amenities average but all working
Would be even nicer with more trees for shade.

We had a really spacious site here, no option except powered so maybe slightly overpriced. In one direction is the beach and the other the bowls club, both less than 10 minutes (bowls club could be less than 2 depending on your site) so the location was perfect. It was quite lively, probably due to it being a Friday night, but nobody around made us feel uneasy. Cleanly facilities in a lovely little town, would stay again if passing back through :)

Great spot and front office people were awesome but the site needs a secure boundary fence . People not using the park short cut through the site  and obviously It’s our fault for not thinking more wisely but I highly suggest locking your van at all times .

Although a pleasant stay a bit of a disappointment in the level of service by reception.
When I booked I informed them that I was picking up a caravan from the dealership and required a site with easy access as I was new to towing. I was allocated a site and was kindly informed that if it wasn't suitable I would be able on the day to choose another site if that wasn't suitable. As the site they chose wasn't easy to access we called reception and ask for a specific site that would have made it a lot easier. We were informed that the park was fully booked and that we could not change sites. Not only was that site empty for the whole time we were there but also five sites beside it we're empty for the whole time. The water and power connection was not central to the 4 sites that shared the facilities. As such our water hose and power cord could not reach. As a result we needed to go to Bunnings to buy what was required to reach the facilities. There were only two grey water drains to service the four sites which were already in use when we arrived. For whatever reason, it was easier for them to say they were fully booked rather than to honour that we would be able to relocate if the site wasn't suitable.
As said, it was a pleasant stay but a disappointing beginning to what should have been an easier setup.
Always a great place too stay when not too busy.
I love this place, toilets are clean, site is clean. We have visited here multiple times, always came back with smiles on our faces.
Lovely all workers

Really love this place. Been here 4 times in the caravan for a Sydney weekend away. Office staff are lovely and helpful. Sites are big and some amenity blocks are old but clean and another brand new. It’s a great spot to walk to the beach and there’s some amazing walks in nearby national parks. We will keep this as a regular haunt.
Last minute booking paid off. Park was well maintained and staff very friendly and helpful. Easy walk to the beach.
Was a nice park however they lost power in one amenities block and water in another yet still charged full price to stay.

Cabin was spacious and staff were excellent.  Major problems with the TV reception.  Foxtel played up and you can't get all the free to air channels.  Very disappointed especially when you pay top price for the accommodation. Council needs to address the TV reception problem.

Absolutely loved staying here it had everything you could want a pool and kids pool also, the nature and wildlife there was cool bush turkeys , rabbits kookaburras so peaceful would highly recommend staying there if you looking for a nice little break from the hustle and bussle oh and it also had a spa bath there which was nice love the cabin vibe and the service and reception was so friendly and welcoming

Would definitely stay there again
Nice caravan park. Amenities could do with some maintenance
Beautiful spot. Bowling club next door with an access gate is convenient. Easy check. Amenities spotless and well maintained
Good:)
Lovely experience, friendly staff, clean and nice rooms. Highly recommend

Day trip to see friends maybe spot a whale or two.
This park is one of the best maintained I've seen, easy caravan parking lots and cabins to hire.
Grounds and Amenities are superb it's only the people tourist that don't give a toss about the next person.
My only let down is that no fires allowed... a small fire pit in the sites would be great especially in winter.
Great park for kids
Very clean amenities. Nothing was too hard. Very friendly people. Close to club for excellent meals. Easy and efficient!
Great spot to camp for the weekend for a family reunion ion
Sights are good size, but uneven. Overall good location
Kurze Wege zum Strand. Saubere Toiletten und Duschen und kurze Wege. Hüpfkissen für Kinder. Pool auch gut. CampKitchen groß und gut. Freundliches Personal.
best time ever
Awesome location friendly staff keep up the great work
Riesiger Campground direkt hinter den Dünen des Soldier Beachs. Wir hatten zu Nebensaison Mitte Februar jede Menge Platz. Toll abends die Stimmung am Strand. Die perfekte Sundowner-Location.

Ever since the management/people who own the place changed there have been many times they messed up with making payments, site booking and have not been helpful in anyway in fixing their mistakes. There has also been a major decrease in games and activities for young kids as there is little to no games or event for them anymore which is disappointing as the place used to be really good
Park clean and dog friendly. 10 min walk to beach, not dog friendly. There is a dog friendly beach further up. Lots of tents.
Good holiday place, families love it. Good access to the pub

Stayed at a power sight with ensuite...location was nice..the ensuite bathroom didn't have many hanging space..the grounds were a little run down..Not really much to do on the premises, the pool is small..no bike hire..for a shirt term stay it's fine

I loved this holiday park. Staff were amazing, even across the weekend they were always available during extended hours, everything i wanted was conveniently located and they had a separate bathroom for children and people living with disability. I can't wait to go back.
Great position. Friendly staff.
Great holiday location for family n friends. Beautiful beaches only 90 minutes from Sydney

Location is great however, the accommodation is not the cleanest. The floors looked like they had never been vacuumed ever. The bathroom was full of with hair everywhere. The beds had hairs in it also. It was extremely off putting. Not a clean feeling at all in the Seagull Cabin.

Managers of park are so friendly and helpful . Location was perfect. We just stayed there this week most days were beautiful except for the last couple which was crazy with the wind and rain . The managers and security guards came around checking on us all . Thankyou all we will definitely be back .
I come here a lot. And love it. So relaxing, but school holidays it is packed. Any holidays really. To really enjoy the area go off peak.
Great location, great park. Quite in Winter, but still good.

We stayed the week prior to Christmas and have to say the park is great! Location is fantastic and the park is kept very clean.

Only feedback (however not negative) is the shower and toilets could do with an upgrade.

Camp kitchen and facilities for kids is great and they have small get togethers with a small one man band etc

We are locals of the central coast and will definitely be back!

A great holiday park for the family. The cabins are amazing, the location is awesome with soldiers beach next door. All the amenities were very clean and the kids ran around in complete safety. Main bed was great, bunk beds could do with new mattresses. The location is awesome with lots of stuff to do and plenty of great cafes nearby. Thoroughly recommend a stay.
Comfortable Accommodation,  reception and location ideal

Absolutely loved our stay it was our first family holiday my 3 year old daughter and 5 year old son loved the pool and jumping pillow my 16 month old twins had so much fun could not fault at all we are definitely coming back
Beautiful position and very quiet park with only a few caravan spots. Amenities spotless.
Can’t give a rating for the rooms as we camped. Sure was good. Kids absolutely love the pool and the air pillow.
Good 👍

Had a great time at Norah head holiday park. The pool is on the smaller side for the size of the park so imagine in peak season it would be not so great for smaller children. We camped in a tent and had great weather but did have a big slope and the back of our site so I would think in bad weather you would have a lot of water running down onto the tent so it is worth checking what the site is like before you book. The staff were great and so helpful.
Staff very friendly and helpful amenities clean and modern
This caravan park is very clean, well maintained, a short walk to the beach. It is right next-door to Norah  Heads Sports Club.
nice and clean close walk to the club great value for money we stay

Great Park, great pool, jumping pillow & park for kids and nice stroll to the water.
Have been many times & will definitely return.
Only visit family that were staying there, their cabin was good, plenty of wildlife including the rabbits 🐰running around everywhere during the day,
Cabin was very clean and modern, was very quiet and loved the sounds of the waves and frogs croaking at night, the only downside was bad phone reception and no WiFi other then that loved our stay :)

Sehr schöner Stellplatz. Preisleistung völlig in Ordnund.
Das Personal ist sehr freundlich.
Die Küche und das Bad sind sehr sauber.
A great spot to stay, beautiful locations, cabins were new and clean. Pool wasn’t heated, camp kitchen is not equipped with cupboards or utensils.

Called at 4.32 pm to book accommodation as we were driving to Sydney and needed a place to stay over....no one answered the phone.
We then arrived at 4.50pm  and the receptionist only after a few minutes decided to acknowledge me.
To be told that we are closed and I have already cashed up, so can't help.  Ok so Google and their website say reception closes at 5pm.
No willingness to help out someone in need of accommodation as it was getting dark.
All I can say is where is the true spirit of genuine Hospitality Service gone these days.

We stayed at Norah Head in January and loved it. So close to the beach, tourist sites, the club next door, the boat ramp, and lots more.
The park itself is well planned and has lots to do. During the school holidays there were activities on in the afternoon/evenings, a pancake van was there most evenings for a sneaky dessert by the pool and coffee vans were there for our early morning caffeine fix. We enjoyed the movie night as a family. The pool area is great for all ages with both a pool and splashpad.
Amenities were modern and even though it was very busy they were kept tidy.
We’ll be back!

Stayed at Norah Head Holiday Park for a couple of nights mid week. Booked a Jabiru cabin. Two bedroomed- can sleep 6.
It has obviously recently under gone a renovation. Beautifully done. Loved the practicality of the large table with bench seats. The room in the living area is tight but otherwise cant fault it. Very reasonably priced at $117 a night for 2 adults and a 16 year old.

The park is huge, but obviously being midweek nights winter, there weren't many around. The facilities all looked to be in good order and the amenities block was spotless, if dated. I can imagine in summer the pool - which is small - would be packed. Its a very short walk to the beach however - and there's a choice of beaches. It was literally a short stroll to the "sporties club" which was nice other than the ridiculous $12 for a small bowl of chips.

I would definitely recommend this park.
Awesome friendly staff. The weather was not the best but we still had a relaxing stay. Amenities were spotless. Will definitely recommend & return for another visit.

Leider wurde uns eine komplette Nacht berechnet obwohl wir nach ein paar Minuten, nachdem wir hier gebucht hatten, gecancelt hatten. Und die Frau an der Rezeption uns auch noch sagte dass das komplette Geld zurück gebucht werden würde.
Beautiful cabin and amazing value for our family . Modern , clean and well equiped . Wish we had time to stay longer .
Great park with very good facilities, lots of room to walk around in the park.

My husband and I and our 2 grand kids absolutely had a wonderful time, we stayed in the bunk house and the kids said it was like camping but with beds and air con, Staff made us feel very welcome and were so helpful, we loved that everything was with in walking distance the club and the beach and awesome restaurants.  We will be coming back with all the family and I will be telling all my friends from home about the great awesome park

Few minutes drive to the light house,
Just remember to book in advance otherwise it might be fully booked.
amazing location
Friendly helpful staff. When booking I was given a choice of 5 huge camp sites at very short notice (45 mins) and the opportunity to check them out first on arrival. Clean modern amenities .
Location is good, amazing spa pool (heated) but was too crowded and untidy during long weekend.
We stayed the long weekend. it was super busy but enjoyable at the same time. Plenty of activities for the kids definitely will be coming back.
Had a drive through powered site for 1 night excellent facilities will be back

Large caravan park, with plenty of sites and cabins directly on the beach. Large sites, great pool and wide streets. Lots of amenities, though the bistro only opened in busy periods. Nice to see a few bunnies hopping around. Cute.  2nd visit here and would definitely recommend.
One of the most naturally beautiful spots close to Sydney - don’t tell too many people! We always love visiting.

Location 10/10
Sites 2/10
Amenities 0/10
So much potential.
Very disappointed

We love staying here and definitely will return soon.. Garden  cabin has well equipped kitchen and beds so comfortable.Nice hot,strong shower and air conditioning with ceiling fans..The lagoon pool plus seperate spas right on the beach were absolutely stunning.
Great location right on the beach, units are a little tired but well equiped

Great affordable cabins, right next to the beach. The locals were a little weird, as I'm not really sure they are welcoming to foreigners. Other than that the cabin space is private and awesome, with a good kitchen setup and amazing views. Also note, whenever there is annoying noisey neighbours in the cabin next door, be ready for a long night of thumping sounds as you can hear the cabins next to you.
Stopped overnight with a caravan was given the most difficult site & looking around with plenty of sites vacant would stay here on the way home but not on site 023

One of my favourite places to holiday but need to return an actual games room that accommodates for teenagers and adults like a pool table and car racing. The outdoor play is very limited for this age group but great for juniours/pre teens.

Great location and staff wonderful...couldn't do enough.  But the Studio Cabin I stayed in was extremely tiny and tired. Mould on the shower screen and wall behind toilet cistern. Shower door keep falling off. Badly in need of capital improvements.

Stayed for a week from 14 Jan 2024 in a caravan. Site was very spacious, close to pool, amenities and camp kitchen.

Showers and toilets always clean and park appears to be very well maintained.

One of the best park we have stayed in for value for money, plenty of and well maintained amenities and very friendly and helpful management and staff.

Highly recommended and will definitely stay there again and will be telling all our friends about this great van park.

Our family had a great stay here we have stayed at many Caravan Parkes and this is one of our favourites. Friendly, helpful staff. The new amenities are great we where close to the pool which the kids loved Only a short walk to the beach. The beach was  amazing! Will be staying here again for sure😃

Loved our stay. Great modern and clean amenities. School holiday activities for the kids. A lot of staff around continuously to keep everything running smoothly. A great place to stay to enjoy Norah head.
Stayed for 5 days had a great time friendly helpfull staff ,modern and clean amenities awesome location
Big thankyou to Korinne for making a very pleasurable stay. Bubbly personality and very accommodating. Will definitely be back sometime.

Very large and very run down.  Terrible amenities not regularly cleaned and too many "out of order" signs.  The beaches are all a long walk and require climbing up and down.    The pool is tiny and completely overrun with children.
Great staff & location, fully enjoyed our stay.
Old amenities. Bathrooms constantly blocked or no hot water. No adult pool. Fair walk to beach (10min) through a dirty carpark.  Pine needles everywhere over our camp site.
Good as always. Facilities are maintained. But the fish man doesn't drive around the park anymore.
We stayed In the pelican cabins and had a great time, newly renovated which was nice. Great staff and and a great park. Will be back next year.

Loved our holiday here! Cabin was homely. Location was perfect- close to beaches, shops and entertainment. Staff were lovely and professional. Our children loved it here- we will be going back and highly recommend to other travellers and families.

there is the most beautiful track walk and beach like 2 min away from our cabins
the entrance is like a 30 min drive there is a nice fishing spot on the rock
there is also a literal rock pool which is kind of mid

there is not much halal food in the area which is a given (except for some indian restaurant)

the actual cabins weren’t the best the cleaners change the bed sheets clean the bathroom and i dont even know if they wiped the kitchen (they 100% dont clean behind the couch) they dont give you new towels or bed sheets so if you’re a clean freak and like everything clean not the place for you but I went with a couple boys and had alot of fun cooking and going to the beach

CABINS:6/10
FOOD IN THE AREA:5/10(not halal)
BEACHES:9/10
SERVICE(not their kindness):1/10

note:not much to do except for the beach so dont say for too long
You will not find a better maintained managed an staffed park the staff are amazing at there job extremely helpful an knowledgeable they have so much pride in there work and happy to help
Rooms weren’t the best. The location was amazing with tones of beaches!
I have visited one or two times every year. When I go there, I always feel good.

This place lack of  maintenance, the bathrooms doors latches, showers paint is flaking.
There was no gate boom at the entrance

I really enjoyed my stay here. Friendly reception, good price and nice accommodation. Heaps of bush turkeys and rabbits. I know the rabbits are a pest species but they are a bit cute hopping around.
I did let a bush turkey bite me a few times trying to get a pat. They don't hurt but it did make me laugh, and I still didn't get to pat it! Well worth a visit.
Absolutely Marvellous service. Relaxing holiday park, within short 15 minute walk of Cafes and scenic Beach views.
Amazing
Schön gelegener Campingplatz direkt beim Strand. Schöne Poolanlage. Freundliche und hilfsbereite Damen am Empfang. Die Sanitäranlagen könnten sauberer und die Kitchen Camp grösser sein.

Rooms were dirty. We were told to whisper on our own balconies by the security guards. We respected the rules and guests of the park however respect was not given back as a guest . security would pick and choose who they approach. There were guests gathering in their driveways drinking alcohol and yet I was asked to go inside in my room and I have to whisper. I have never been so disrespected as a guest . I have been coming for 15 years and I have never seen such disrespect and unfair treatment by the staff. All guests are lovely however the staff and the cleanliness is a below grade rating.
****** Definitely not coming back******  I will be informing all my family friends and community to not be going there again.
Very expensive, dirty toilets and really full

First things first — the room was filthy. Sand all over the floors, hair on the floors, and the bedsheets had clearly not been changed. Pillowcases were stained and dirty (photos attached). The kitchen supplies were also unclean. This is completely unacceptable and honestly disgusting for a place charging holiday rates.

The issues didn’t stop there. The night security staff were extremely rude and selective in who they chose to approach. There were groups drinking, partying and playing loud music with speakers until 3am with no action taken, yet our group was repeatedly approached for simply talking quietly on our balconies. We were even told to turn off our TV inside our own cabin.

The way the security staff spoke to children was completely inappropriate. Our kids were told to leave the pool before closing time (even though they just wanted to stay until the last minute, which is completely normal), and were then told, “that’s okay, you can all just die in there when the chlorine starts pumping.” As a result, the children were scared to go back into the pool the following day, genuinely believing they could get hurt or die. Speaking to children in this manner is unacceptable and disturbing.

We were constantly reminded about a “contract” regarding noise after 11pm. Noise, to us, means parties, loud music, shouting and disruption — not families quietly talking on balconies. If contracts matter so much, then one would expect the rooms to at least meet basic cleanliness standards.

It became very clear that our group — and another family simply playing cards on their balcony — were being singled out. We do not drink, we do not party, and we were never disrespectful. Meanwhile, there were other children screaming swear words in shared amenities, banging on walls late at night, and causing genuine disturbance — which staff completely ignored.

After coming to this park for over 15 years, we know the difference in standards — and the decline is obvious. To then see management publicly respond to reviews by blaming guests and making false claims about disrespectful behaviour instead of addressing genuine concerns is beyond rude and deeply unprofessional.

I would not spend another dollar at this place. When families save and work hard to go away at the end of the year, they should not arrive to filthy rooms, feel targeted, or be intimidated for simply spending quality time together.

Do not waste your money here. Until cleanliness, management attitude, and staff conduct are addressed, this place does not deserve its reputation on these reviews.

The attached photos show the condition of just one of the rooms upon arrival and are not representative of all rooms in the property. The floors were visibly filthy, with dirt and debris throughout, including a cut piece of someone’s hair left on the floor. The mop bucket contained dirty brown water when we arrived, clearly indicating the floors had not been cleaned prior to check-in. There was heavy dust accumulation on the staircase and surfaces, and the bedding was stained and unclean. This level of hygiene is completely unacceptable and falls well below basic accommodation and cleaning standards.

We have been coming to blue lagoon for a very long time but unfortunately this year will be our last. The cleanliness of the rooms are disgusting hair, sand and dust all over the floors window seals and sheets 🤮 The smell of mould in the rooms are bad. If you take a family cabin be sure to bring up your ownt pots pans and bowls as what they offer are soooo small and no where near enough if you are a family. The play ground is sooo old and damaged I don't even believe it's safe for the kids to use. The female security guard walking around at night honestly makes you feel like your a kid back in school on detention with constant hassle about noise curfew which is fine but if people are sitting on their balcony chit chatting at the end of the night surely you should be allowed on a holiday that people work so hard for to go on and enjoy not be hassled. The way she approached everyone and spoke to even this kids is absolutely appalling and unacceptable. I won't ever be going there which is a pitty as the location is beautiful but unfortunately the cleanliness and the security guard have put me off as for the money we pay there are alot more nicer holiday parks with more facilities and Soo clean  for the same price. Very disappointed customer. P.S yes when I complained they sent cleaners in the next day but was still not cleaned properly they only addresses the flooring issue and the main bed sheet but the dust was still in the cabin. Also it's very inconvenient when you have someone coming into your cabin to clean it after you have already moved in with all your things when it should have been spotless before I arrived the day before.

Property maintenance all round is lacking. Bathrooms and showers are terrible.
Femal security on site intimidates and harrassment of kids constantly is disgusting.
Front desk service is great.
Location to beach is great.
Studio cabins are too small.
Text sites are uneven and super sandy.

We recently stayed here on a caravan site for a staycation and had a great experience. The location is second to none — right on the beachfront and within easy walking distance of cafés, restaurants, and shops. It’s the perfect spot for families, especially with kids.
The facilities were fairly standard but well maintained and clean. The resort itself really comes to life during the holidays, with food vans and a good range of activities on offer. Overall, we really enjoyed our stay and would happily come back again.
Great place to holiday. Very convenient location and family friendly

We want on a Family holiday January 2025. We are just about to go back next week.
It was great location PERFECT view amazing. Have recommended it to all friends and family.
Great for all ages.
Great location, great facilities and staff are very informative and accomodating. We had a group booking and the staff were fantastic. Highly recommend.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
We had an amazing stay at Blue Lagoon in the 2-bedroom ocean view cabin (Cabin 34), and honestly, we couldn’t have asked for a better place to escape the stresses of day-to-day life.

The view of the beach was absolutely stunning. You could sit on the veranda and watch the waves crashing in, and being so close to the ocean meant you could even hear the waves while lying in bed — the perfect sound to fall asleep to.

The cabin itself was fantastic (OMG 😍), and the water pressure in the shower was to die for! The staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming, which made the stay even better.

Highly recommend Blue Lagoon — we’re already looking forward to booking again!

Expensive accommodation for extremely run down cabins with no ocean view despite the name and unmaintained facilities.  The kiosk was closed, the after hours key box was broken, the pool was so dirty, the spa heating was broken.  The staff were very pleasant but nothing seemed to be working for them.

This place was really on point and absolutely perfect for us. I read some of the reviews before we went saying it was a bit ‘retro’. It may have been in some ways with the old school permanent caravan homes, but it had everything we needed. We weren’t expecting a 5 star resort, we just wanted a family friendly holiday park and that’s exactly what we got. I went with my 2 children and we camped in a tent near the Norah Head bowling club. I expected it to be noisy, didn’t hear a thing. Bonus was there was a path to walk to the club not far from us. Facilities were perfect, very accessible. The kids loved the jumping pillow, pool with attached water park, and the playground. They had outdoor movie nights with a pancake van (score!), even an ice cream truck that drove around the park both days (boy he must make a killing in the holidays). Chatting with one of the owners he mentioned they had even had a band that had played at the club come over during the Christmas holidays, and they’re looking to do more of the same. It had everything we wanted to enjoy ourselves, this place is staying on the top of our must visit again list. If you want a nice simple, affordable holiday park, this place should be on your list. I recommend visiting the Norah head lighthouse as well, it was a 2 min drive from the park. Looked awesome at night as well.

The pool was great and lots of kids loved the karts  you could hire and ride around.||Maybe a five - ten minute walk to the beach and a  short drive to the lighthouse. |We definitely would stay again.

We camped here over the Labour Day weekend with two sites + 1 cabin and while it wasn’t a terrible experience overall, there are a few important things future visitors should be aware of.

One key difference from other holiday parks we’ve stayed at is that this site has full-time residents living there. It created a slightly uncomfortable atmosphere, as it often felt like we were encroaching on their space rather than enjoying a typical holiday park environment. It’s not necessarily a deal-breaker, but it’s worth knowing ahead of time.

Another issue was that there were rules not listed on the website — particularly a strict one-car-per-site policy. We had two campsites with multiple guests, and only being allowed one car per site was a real hassle. It would’ve helped if this was made clearer before booking.

The staff were constantly patrolling, which we understand can be for safety, but it felt a bit overbearing. They frequently called people out for minor issues, which made it hard to fully relax.

Lastly, our campsite had a persistent sewage smell — strong enough to wake us up at night. This may have just been our section of the park, but it really impacted our stay.

This campground has potential, but clearer communication, a more welcoming environment, and better site maintenance would go a long way.

The roads are another ongoing issue. There are many potholes, and despite being told multiple times that they would be fixed, nothing has changed.

Awesome location — cafés within walking distance (Bateau Bay Café was our fave!), Wyrrabalong Coastal Walk and Shelly Beach close by, and 3 Peppers Pizza was a hit for dinner. The kids had a blast — we’ll definitely be back! 😊🌴

Loved the beachside ca ins very clean and comfortable wifi worked cheap laundry facilities loved the pool and slide. Plenty of bbqs around the park to.

We stayed in the loft cabin which was a good size for us, but the walls attached must be quite thin as we heard lots of noise of the stairs and constant bunk bed climbing of the room next door (may have been very loud neighbours). Great location, lovely individual cabins with the comforts of home, fantastic for a family holiday without costing an arm or a leg. Lots of amenities on site for easy access. Water park & pool on site for the kids ( & adults) to have fun & the beach is right across the road. Only a short drive from Bateau Bay & The Entrance if you want to sightsee.

Absolutely loved our stay!! The staff are fantastic to deal with, both while booking & on our arrival, great customer service. We were in one of the beach walk cabins & it was awesome hearing the waves at night. My kids loved walking down to the beach each morning. Very happy with the location, short drives to any tourist attractions we wanted to do. Great in-house facilities too, with the kids braving the cold to test out the water park. It’s pretty close to the local shopping centre & such a nice touch to be able to get members prices for dinner at the Bateau Bay bowling club for a nice night out!! The cabin was a good size for our family, the only concern we had was the placement of the bunks upstairs because our little boy sometimes sleep walks in an unknown environment but it was easily managed.  Definitely recommend to anyone who wants a home away from home experience while on holidays, we’ll definitely be back!!

Stayed only one night for a funeral and it wasn’t a very pleasant experience. The cabins are so dusty and gross. There was a dead cockroach upon entering the cabin and had a horrible musty smell. You would think someone wouldn’t done another clean when expecting someone to stay in there. My allergies were horrible with the amount of dust. My feet were black after taking my shoes off and walking around. The shower doesn’t even turn off properly and keeps running even when I turned the taps off all the way. The main room downstairs is also extremely cold as the heater doesn't reach down the hall. Walls are so thin I can hear people stomping and running all night. Overall not a great experience. I bet one of the newer cabins would’ve been nicer had it been available. The old cabins where the beach is need to be upgraded and cleaned properly.

We stayed in a cabin that was just a short walk to the beach – such a bonus! It was lovely hearing the waves at night, so relaxing. The cabin itself was clean and had everything we needed, including a well-equipped kitchen. Customer service was also great – really helpful and kind.

A couple of things to note: the floorboards were quite squeaky, which made it a bit noisy at night, and the main bedroom was very cold as the heater didn’t seem to reach it. Also, while the cabin was tidy, it could use a deeper clean – the lamps and window sills were quite dusty. The walls are also super thin, there were lots of random loud noises during the night and you can hear everything going on outside. Unfortunately we can't control other people but some consideration goes a long way.

Still, overall it was a great stay and we enjoyed being so close to the beach!
Great caravan park, kids love the water park and slide, pool. Close to Shelly beach ⛱️ very good
Neat, tidy clean and friendly.

- Booking and Check In
Absolutely pleasant experiance room was "ready" to check in on time and on the occasion that I called to make enquiries and such in the lead up to our stay, staff were very helpful and informative

- Cabin
Upon check in the room was filthy. Walls had multiple marks, scrapes and filthy. Stove top was greasy and floors were horrible. Air Conditioner was full of mould. Quilts on bed had no quilt covers and room fans were full of dust, peeling paint and rust. Stairs leading to upstairs loft bedroom were sharp and could only be described as a death trap all inclusive with rusty exposed nails on the side. Bunk beds in loft room were broken and slats on the bed were bolted to a large peice of board that was bigger than the bed leading to sharp slats coming out past the bed frame which my son scraped his legs on multiple occasions getting in and out of the bed.
All other cabins had an outdoor seating table except for ours. Bathroom is extremely narrow however was relatively clean. External room light on balcony did not work and light at bottom of stairs was held together by tape and had sharp exposed metal. Included WiFi - not existent.

- Waterpark and pool
Absolutely phenomenal. No complaints at all. This was the highlight of our stay and aside from the staff the only reason for my two star rating.

- Car park and Park security
Very limited lighting throught the park especially in the car park where our cabins were leading to my son tripping over when coming and going due to uneven ground and nil lighting. Rear pedestrian gate rarely locked which was a safety concern that was raised during our stay however not rectified. Signage around driving through the caravan park was near non existent leading to multiple cars going in the wrong direction when coming and going.

Overall our stay was definitely not worth $399 per night and we will not be returning again.

One of the best caravan parks to stop and rest while crossing the border. The facilities are well-maintained, especially the toilets and showering area. The environment is peaceful, making it an ideal spot for a relaxing break during your journey.

We recently stayed at Shelly Beach Accommodation Park with over 40 family members, booking 10 cabins for 5 days. Admittedly, we had read the reviews beforehand, and while they weren’t overly raving, we decided to book on good faith — and we’re so glad we did!

From the start, everything exceeded our expectations. The rooms were clean and tidy, and the coastal cabins were incredibly convenient for our group. The location, so close to the beach, was absolutely phenomenal and made our stay even more enjoyable.

A special mention goes to the night security guard, who was an absolute pleasure and made us feel safe and welcome every evening.

Not a single family member had a complaint — everyone thoroughly enjoyed themselves. We couldn’t fault a thing and would highly recommend Shelly Beach Accommodation Park for large family gatherings or anyone looking for a relaxing coastal getaway. We’ll definitely be back!

I was initially hesitant to book at Shelly Beach Holiday Park after reading some negative reviews, but we decided to give it a chance for our end of year family holiday. We booked 10 cabins, and to be honest, it turned out to be a great experience!

The customer service was excellent, and even though we were a big group making some noise, it was never an issue, which was a relief compared to other places. The cabins were decent, nothing fancy. The upstairs beds and room was small, and bathrooms were quite narrow, but everything else made up for it.

The location was perfect, the park was clean, and the bins were emptied daily. The splash park and pool were a hit with everyone, and the overall vibe was fantastic. Having access to the beach was amazing and so convienent,  Security was also great, and the price was reasonable.

Overall, I’d stay here again!

By far our favourite caravan park. We're a family with older kids and a caravan. This is our default central Coast park. It's dog friendly and so is the adjacent beach plus shops in walking distance. Pricing is fabulous.
With the heavy rain we had half the park was under water,the council needs to do a lot of planning to fix this park.

This was our second visit to this park and we're already planning our next one.

Friendly, accommodating staff make this a venue we want to return to having started a few times in the last year or so. Having lived in the vicinity, it's lovely to revisit this lovely area. We'll be back!

On last morning I asked reception if we could use it as it seemed odd - the receptionist seemed oblivious and called the maintenance guy who said yes - it was almost like they didn't bother to open it each day.
The description promised Foxtel and wifi in the cabin - absolutely no wifi connectivity and 1 Foxtel channel - totally disappointing and not as advertised. Beds average and of the 10 pillows in the cabin, only one wasnt stiff and too tall. Staff seemed angry/ sharp at both interactions we had and weren't welcoming. Smoking being allowed on balconies that drifts onto your eating area was not great and "path" to beach has eroded wood that has turned into hurdles - not easily accessible. Overall, it seemed like an okay park and we liked the location for what we needed nearby. We wouldn't rush back to an Oceanview Beachcomber cabin that over promises and under delivers.

Firstly, we are long standing nrma members and we were not advised of the change and have been advised now we cant use our discount on our current stay even though we paid in full after the change over.

Secondly: from our last stay to this we have noticed a decline in maintenance. The amenities on the cnr of 15th and 17th needs some urgent tlc. Doors don’t lock, soap dispensers don’t work, and hygiene overall seems lacking. According to the cleanliness sign off sheet it appears only 1 clean and a spot check - this is not enough in peak season. I query if any park staff actually care to visit just to understand the lack of cleanliness and hygiene between these cleans.  Reinstate the outdoor beach showers to avoid dirt/sand build up in the shower block.

Overall room for improvement especially from a large commercial private chain now titled as NRMA. Saying all, we will be back.
